findings.evidence_quote: Cámbiate a TurboTax Do It Yourself y declara sin cargo en la app hasta el 28 de febrero. [Consulta los detalles de la oferta](https://blog.turbotax.intuit.com/max-refund-npd-r1-es/#offer-details)
findings.location: mobile app '$0' offer section ('Presenta tu declaración por $0 en la aplicación')
findings.reasoning: The rule triggers whenever TurboTax Free is mentioned. This page has a section headlined 'Presenta tu declaración por $0 en la aplicación' offering to file for $0 ('declara sin cargo') via the app, which is a free-filing offer analogous to TurboTax Free. The rule requires the ~37% qualification/eligibility disclosure (with the simple Form 1040 limitation language) to appear directly underneath this mention. Instead, the page only links to 'Consulta los detalles de la oferta' (offer details), with no visible ~37%/simple-1040 disclosure text anywhere on the page, not even in the footer. I considered the counter-reading that linking to an offer-details page could satisfy the rule by reference, but the rule explicitly requires the disclosure to be disclosed 'right underneath' the mention, not merely linked elsewhere or hidden behind a modal. Since the required text is entirely absent from the visible page content, axis A is not satisfied. No approved library wording appears either, so axis B is false/na intersecting into an unapproved_violation. This is the clearest violation on the page.

Rule R-01 triggers whenever TurboTax Free is mentioned. On this page, the footer navigation includes the link text “Free Edition tax filing,” which is a reference to the TurboTax Free product. That is sufficient to meet the trigger condition, even though the reference is in a nav/footer rather than the hero body copy.

Once triggered, the rule requires that the specific disclosure “~37% of filers qualify. Simple Form 1040 returns only (no schedules, except for EITC, CTC, student loan interest, and Schedule 1-A).” appear right underneath the TurboTax Free mention. Reviewing the provided page text, there is no occurrence of that disclosure or any equivalent eligibility statement anywhere on the page, including near the footer link. Thus axis A (requirement present and properly placed) is not satisfied.

Axis B asks whether the wording matches the approved library entry D-01. Because no eligibility disclosure appears at all, there is nothing to compare, so the wording does not match the approved text, and axis B is false.

A potential counter-argument is that a bare navigation link to “Free Edition tax filing” might be treated as exempt or that the disclosure could live on the destination page instead of on this blog page. However, the rule as given is explicit and absolute: if TurboTax free is mentioned on the page, the disclosure must be directly underneath that mention. There is no exception carved out for footer/nav links. Therefore this page is non-compliant under a strict reading of R-01.

Trigger met because the page explicitly mentions TurboTax Free Edition via the link text, which counts as a mention of TurboTax free. The rule requires the specific eligibility disclosure to appear right underneath; no such disclosure (~37% qualify + Simple 1040 only with listed exceptions) appears underneath or elsewhere on the page. Therefore Axis A fails; since nothing close to the approved wording appears, Axis B also fails. Counter-reading considered: this could be just navigational and not promotional, but the rule does not exempt navigation items; it triggers on any mention.

Trigger met because the page makes a free claim about TurboTax (“Start for Free”). The required Free eligibility disclosure is not placed right underneath or anywhere visible on the page. Thus Axis A fails; there is no approved-text match, so Axis B fails. Counter-reading: one might argue this CTA refers to trying the product at no cost and not the Free Edition SKU, but the rule applies broadly to mentions of TurboTax free to ensure appropriate eligibility context.

findings.evidence_quote: Presenta tu declaración por $0 en la aplicación
findings.location: mobile app promo section (“Cámbiate a TurboTax …”)
findings.reasoning: Trigger met because the page makes a $0/free claim for filing in the app. The required disclosure (~37% qualify; Simple 1040 only with specified exceptions) is not provided right underneath or elsewhere. Axis A fails; no match to approved wording, so Axis B fails. Counter-reading: this may be a limited-time promotional $0 offer distinct from the Free Edition, but the rule requires the Free eligibility disclosure whenever TurboTax free is mentioned, and no such disclosure is present.
